brief_notes: All numerical claims in the generated paper are accurate and match the source extraction exactly. PCC values for all three architectures (ResNet, SWIN, LSTM) in both causal and non-causal conditions are correct. All eight p-values are correct. Sample sizes (N=48, n=32/16, n=5/43) are correct. Speech parameter PCCs (voice weight 0.834, loudness 0.545, pitch 0.907, f1 0.827, f2 0.895) are all correct. Hemisphere comparison values (ResNet right PCC=0.790, SWIN right PCC=0.781) and all STOI+ p-values match. Training hyperparameters and all technical specifications are accurate. No hallucinated numbers found. The paper is well-organized with clear tables and good academic prose. Completeness is slightly docked because some details from the source (e.g., specific word-level cross-validation fold details, some synthesizer mathematical details) are summarized rather than fully reproduced, but all major findings are covered. Citations are reasonable but limited in number relative to the source paper's reference list. Presentation is good with well-formatted tables, though it lacks figures.
